workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Can you extract the deployment blockers from this exec meeting notes for Parnell Aerospace? This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 08:42 EST
Subject: Re: Re: deployment blockers Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-01-14 11:30 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Parnell Aerospace

There's been a change in our timeline for deployment blockers. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Parnell Aerospace: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-D trap (MSX writeback proposed without evidence in thread); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-D failure mode (MSX writeback proposed without evidence in thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-D -- MSX writeback proposed without evidence in thread
